prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response:
# What a CPU Is

Oh sweetie, well, you know how your brain tells your body what to do? Well, a CPU is like the brain of the computer! It's a teeny tiny thing inside there that makes all the decisions and tells everything else what to do. All those letters you type and pictures you look at? The CPU is working away, figuring it all out. It's very clever, though I must say I don't understand half of what it does - it all happens so fast! The important thing is that without that little brain in there, your computer wouldn't be able to do anything at all, dear.
